opp.name: TEGNA_S2P_Apr'2026
opp.next_step: 6/3/26 MQ Reaching out to Arvind to schedule discovery and understand what their procurement process looks like post-merger. 5/18/26 MQ Reach out to Arvind first week of June to check status of Merger with Nextdoor and plan for procurement dept. structure of combined entity. Schedule a follow-up meeting for ~2 months out (as Arvind suggested) and position it as a focused discussion on supplier onboarding and manual analytics ROI, not a generic full-suite demo. Before that meeting, send brief materials on Zycus’ onboarding and spend analytics capabilities and ask Arvind for 1–2 data points (e.g., supplier onboarding volume, time spent on analytics) to tailor an ROI view based on questionairre.
opp.ais_why:
• TEGNA is already leveraging AI apps for invoicing and account payments as part of post-merger transformation with Nexstar. Active AP-adjacent AI deployment lines up with AP Smart Desk.
• Buyer Arvind wants quantified ROI on manual analytics rather than a generic full-suite demo. Pragmatic AI buyer asking for value math.
• Opportunity created April 2026 with recent activity. AI-driven AP transformation is core to the merger thesis, keeping the AI conversation warm even without an Avoma call yet.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
Next_Step__c: 'ask Arvind for 1–2 data points (e.g., supplier onboarding volume, time spent on analytics) to tailor an ROI view based on questionairre'
metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed across three operational areas but not yet quantified. Arvind stated on 7 April 2026 that TEGNA's iClient CLM system 'has several issues and is far from perfect', that supplier onboarding in Oracle 'does leave something to be desired', and that spend analytics 'is all done manually' via spreadsheets. Transformation pain is also present: 'There is a lot of pain in transformation. So we're running transformation across various functions... each of these come with its own challenges... it's a change management uphill climb.' However, no quantified metrics were captured (e.g., supplier onboarding cycle time, FTE hours on manual analytics, contract redline turnaround, procurement request-to-fulfillment time). Arvind's decision lens is ROI-driven: 'if there is an ROI we can drive, and increase sourcing productivity, yeah, certainly' and 'It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build.' The next step is to gather 1-2 data points (supplier onboarding volume, time spent on analytics) to tailor an ROI view, per Next_Step__c. Quantifying the value case is the immediate gap blocking a credible business case.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
Next_Step__c: '6/3/26 MQ Reaching out to Arvind to schedule discovery' (rep-initiated, not buyer-pulled)
champion.narrative: Arvind Swamy (Head of Procurement & Strategic Sourcing) is a developing champion with positive sentiment but no confirmed advocacy or multi-threading. On 7 April 2026, Arvind demonstrated genuine interest: attended Zycus' Procurement AI World event in Atlanta despite a 'tough schedule' (per business development associate), engaged in a 30-minute discovery call, articulated pain points and AI appetite, and explicitly requested a 2-month circle-back aligned to the merger timeline ('let's circle back in two months'). He has prior Zycus exposure ('we use Zycus when I was at NCR... very early stages') and familiarity with Ariba and JAGGAER, giving him informed buyer credibility. However, he has not demonstrated champion behaviors: no internal introductions (EB, CFO, Legal, InfoSec), no shared evaluation criteria or BRD, no competitive intel (he did not volunteer who else TEGNA is considering), and no proactive push to keep Zycus engaged during the freeze. His role and retention post-merger are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). If Arvind is displaced or his function is absorbed into Nexstar's procurement org, Zycus loses its only thread. The next step (early August 2026 post-merger call) must assess: (1) whether Arvind survived the merger and retains decision influence, (2) whether he will advocate for Zycus internally and introduce the Economic Buyer, and (3) whether he will share Nexstar's S2P strategy and competitive landscape. Until then, he is a friendly contact but not yet a confirmed champion.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
competition.narrative: Competition is confirmed: JAGGAER and Ariba are known alternatives, both from Arvind's prior experience rather than an active TEGNA evaluation. On 7 April 2026, Arvind stated: 'From a Zycus perspective, you know, we use Zycus when I was at NCR... I have used Ariba. I have used JAGGAER, etcetera, and a little bit of Zycus. Over time. So I understand the scope and what these tools can do for us.' This is historical familiarity, not a live competitive shortlist. No evidence that TEGNA is actively evaluating JAGGAER or Ariba today, and no mention of Coupa, GEP, or other S2P vendors. The incumbent stack is Oracle Fusion ERP (Punch-Out Checkout for eProcurement) and iClient CLM, which are acknowledged as painful but not explicitly being replaced ('we have Orca Fusion for ERP. We have iClient as a CLM, and that's all we have'). Post-merger, Nexstar's S2P stack becomes the wildcard: if Nexstar has an incumbent vendor (Ariba, JAGGAER, Coupa, or another), that vendor may be imposed on the combined entity, eliminating a competitive process entirely. The wedge is Zycus' AI differentiation (Merlin agentic platform, guided onboarding, iRisk, autonomous negotiation agent, orchestration in Teams) and integrated suite positioning ('one house, one solution, one throat to choke') versus cobbled point solutions. Arvind acknowledged the one-stop-shop value ('There is benefit to do the one stop shop') but made ROI and cost the decisive factors ('It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build'). The August 2026 post-merger discovery call must uncover Nexstar's S2P incumbent and whether TEGNA will run a competitive RFP or inherit Nexstar's vendor.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed across three operational areas, owned by Arvind Swamy (Head of Procurement & Strategic Sourcing). On 7 April 2026, Arvind identified: (1) CLM friction: 'The current Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) system has several issues and is far from perfect', indicating contract authoring, approval, and repository inefficiencies. (2) Supplier onboarding friction: when asked how onboarding is done, Arvind said 'Onboarding is done in Oracle' and acknowledged it 'does leave something to be desired', signaling a clunky, form-based process without guided workflow or risk monitoring. (3) Manual analytics: 'Do you do analytics? It's all done manually'—confirmed via spreadsheets, meaning no real-time spend visibility, category insights, or savings tracking. (4) Transformation change-management pain: 'There is a lot of pain in transformation. So we're running transformation across various functions... each of these come with its own challenges... it's a change management uphill climb.' Arvind is running concurrent transformations across sourcing, procurement, market tech, and broadcast tech, stretching resources and slowing adoption. The pain is operational (time, manual effort, fragmented tools) rather than financial/quantified, but it is real and acknowledged. The merger freeze has paused transformation execution ('we can't sign anything, we can't move anything'), but the pain persists and will re-emerge post-acquisition.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026 (no contracting discussion)
Account contacts: WILLIAM T HERMAN (Assistant General Counsel) — not engaged
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, InfoSec, procurement channel) is entirely unknown. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, or InfoSec questionnaire has been discussed. The 7 April 2026 discovery call did not cover contracting mechanics, and no subsequent activity has advanced this. For a full-suite S2P implementation, TEGNA will require legal review (WILLIAM T HERMAN, Assistant General Counsel, is an account contact but not engaged), InfoSec approval (common for SaaS procurement platforms with supplier data and integrations), and likely a phased SOW covering implementation, data migration from the relevant stakeholder / iClient CLM, and integration with the relevant stakeholder (Arvind confirmed Teams usage). The merger adds complexity: Nexstar may impose its own contracting standards, MSA templates, or procurement approval workflows. Post-merger discovery (early August 2026) must surface: (1) who leads legal and InfoSec review for the combined entity, (2) whether Nexstar has an existing Zycus MSA or prefers a new contract, (3) procurement approval thresholds (a $110k SaaS deal may require CFO or exec sponsor sign-off), and (4) expected contract negotiation timeline and redline cycles. Until the merger resolves, no paper-process visibility is possible.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026 (Arvind Swamy, Head of Procurement, no EB present)
Account contacts: Victoria Harker (CFO), Daniel Caldwell (VP Financial Shared Services) — not engaged
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not yet identified. The discovery call on 7 April 2026 was with Arvind Swamy, Head of Procurement & Strategic Sourcing, who is driving the sourcing and procurement transformation but has not demonstrated budget authority or C-level sponsorship. No CFO, VP Finance, or executive sponsor has appeared in any conversation, and no Contact Role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists on the opportunity. Arvind referenced the merger uncertainty and noted 'we're kind of in a holding period where we can't sign anything, we can't move anything', indicating he is not the final commercial decision-maker. The account-level contact list includes Victoria Harker (Executive VP & CFO) and other C-level executives (Nicole Keach, Chief Digital Officer & CTO; Lynn Beall, EVP & COO Media Operations), but none have engaged. For a $110k S2P transformation deal, CFO or VP Finance sign-off is expected. The next discovery call (early August 2026 post-merger) must map the approval chain and secure an introduction to the Economic Buyer, particularly if the combined TEGNA-Nexstar entity changes the budget authority structure.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
Next_Step__c: 'understand what their procurement process looks like post-merger... check status of Merger with Nextdoor and plan for procurement dept. structure of combined entity'
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is on hold due to the TEGNA-Nexstar merger legal limbo and will not be known until post-acquisition clarity (expected early August 2026). On 7 April 2026, Arvind stated: 'We're in a legal limbo... the federal court in Sacramento has put a hold on it... we're kind of in a holding period where we can't sign anything, we can't move anything... I would give it at least two months because we don't know where we're gonna land. And then the size of the may be bigger.' The process is paused at the buyer's request, not due to Zycus inaction. No approval chain, procurement governance, or vendor selection framework has been mapped. Arvind is driving transformation across sourcing, procurement, and travel, but whether he will retain his role and function post-merger is uncertain ('they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). The first post-merger discovery call (scheduled for early August 2026 per Next_Step__c) must uncover: (1) who owns the S2P decision for the combined entity (TEGNA procurement, Nexstar procurement, or a new shared-services structure), (2) whether a competitive RFP will be required, (3) approval levels and sign-off chain, and (4) Nexstar's existing S2P stack and any incumbent vendor lock. Until then, the process is unknown and appropriately paused.

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy (buyer)
Next_Step__c: 'position it as a focused discussion on supplier onboarding and manual analytics ROI, not a generic full-suite demo'
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are partially surfaced but not formalized. On the 7 April 2026 call, Arvind articulated three evaluation lenses: (1) strong ROI and measurable productivity gains ('if there is an ROI we can drive, and increase sourcing productivity'), (2) preference for an integrated one-stop-shop solution over point-solution integration headaches ('There is benefit to do the one stop shop. It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build'), and (3) high interest in AI capabilities to enhance sourcing and procurement processes ('I would say so. Yes.' when asked about AI interest level). Zycus positioned Merlin agentic AI, guided onboarding, iRisk supplier monitoring, and orchestration embedded in Microsoft Teams; Arvind acknowledged the positioning and noted familiarity with Zycus, JAGGAER, and Ariba from prior roles but said he is 'not really that up to speed on' current feature functionality. No formal RFP, BRD, or weighted scorecard has been shared. The criteria are inferred from stated preferences, not confirmed through a structured evaluation framework. Post-merger, TEGNA's criteria may shift if Nexstar imposes a preferred vendor or standardized procurement stack. The August 2026 re-engagement must confirm whether TEGNA will run a competitive RFP and what Nexstar's S2P strategy is.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Arvind Swamy confirmed high AI interest on the 7 April 2026 discovery call ('I would say so. Yes.' when asked if AI interest is relatively high). He is driving transformation across sourcing, procurement, and travel, and explicitly stated 'if there is an ROI we can drive, and increase sourcing productivity, yeah, certainly', signaling AI as a productivity lever. Zycus positioned Merlin agentic AI (guided onboarding, iRisk, orchestration in Teams, autonomous negotiation agent), and Arvind engaged positively without skepticism. TEGNA's pain points (manual analytics, clunky onboarding, CLM friction) map well to AI-powered solutions. No AIS score in Salesforce, but call evidence places TEGNA as AI Curious leaning toward AI Hungry (interest confirmed, ROI-driven, open to AI demos post-merger).

items.change: Opportunity created (TEGNA_S2P_Apr'2026, $110k, close 16 March 2027, Products: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin)
Discovery call with Arvind Swamy: established transformation scope, pain (CLM, onboarding, manual analytics), high AI interest, 2-month circle-back window due to TEGNA-Nexstar merger freeze
Rep reached out to Arvind to schedule post-merger discovery and understand combined-entity procurement structure (Next_Step__c, awaiting buyer reply)

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 9 April 2026 (2 days after discovery call), qualified same cycle. Rep reached out 3 June 2026 (23 days ago) to schedule post-merger discovery, aligned to Arvind's 2-month circle-back request from 7 April call. No buyer reply yet, but within the agreed holding-pattern window (merger resolution expected early August 2026). Next milestone is post-merger discovery call early August 2026 to assess procurement structure, Nexstar S2P strategy, and Arvind's role/influence post-acquisition.

items.detail: TEGNA-Nexstar merger legal limbo (federal court hold in Sacramento) has frozen all procurement decisions and contract signings. Merger resolution expected early August 2026 (2 months from 7 April call), but timeline is uncertain. If merger is blocked or delayed further, deal timeline slips indefinitely.

Arvind Swamy's post-merger role and function are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). If Nexstar displaces him or absorbs TEGNA procurement into its own org, Zycus loses its only champion and must re-establish contact with Nexstar's procurement leadership.

Nexstar's S2P incumbent vendor (if any) is unknown. If Nexstar has a locked relationship with Ariba, JAGGAER, Coupa, or another vendor, that platform may be imposed on TEGNA, eliminating a competitive selection and making this deal unwinnable.

No Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance) engaged; budget authority and approval chain unmapped. Arvind acknowledged cost and ROI as decision drivers ('It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build'), but no CFO sponsorship or budget confirmation secured.
Single-threaded: only Arvind Swamy has engaged. No Legal, InfoSec, IT, Finance, or C-level stakeholders introduced. If Arvind loses influence or leaves, no alternative thread exists.

No quantified ROI metrics captured (supplier onboarding cycle time, FTE hours on manual analytics, contract turnaround). Arvind's decision lens is ROI-driven, but no baseline data provided to build a credible business case.

items: Discovery call 7 April 2026 (80 days ago) with Arvind Swamy established pain, AI interest, and explicit 2-month circle-back window.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and appropriately paused due to external buyer-imposed merger freeze. Arvind Swamy requested a 2-month circle-back (early June 2026, now early August 2026 post-merger), and rep actioned that window on 3 June 2026. No buyer reply yet, but within the agreed holding period. No evidence of cooling; the silence is merger-driven, not disinterest. Next milestone is post-merger discovery call early August 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 9 April 2026, qualified same cycle, 78 days in Qualified stage as of 26 June 2026. Close date 16 March 2027 is 263 days out. Discovery call 7 April 2026 (80 days ago) established transformation scope, pain points, AI interest, and 2-month follow-up window. Rep reached out 3 June 2026 (23 days ago, within the agreed holding period) to schedule post-merger discovery. No forward slip; the March 2027 close accommodates the known 2-month merger freeze plus a 6-8 month enterprise S2P evaluation and implementation planning cycle. Pace is appropriate for a Qualified-stage transformation deal with a known external gate (merger resolution expected early August 2026). Buyer explicitly requested circle-back in two months from 7 April call, placing next milestone early June 2026; rep actioned that window on 3 June. Deal is live and on the planned cadence.
evidence: Discovery call 7 April 2026 with Arvind Swamy established transformation scope, pain (CLM issues, manual analytics, onboarding friction), high AI interest, andProducts scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin).

Arvind explicitly stated 'let's circle back in two months' due to TEGNA-Nexstar merger legal limbo (federal court hold in Sacramento), preventing any procurement decisions or contract signings until merger clarity (expected resolution timeframe: approximately 2 months from 7 April, early June 2026).
Rep reached out 3 June 2026 (23 days ago, aligned to the 2-month window) to schedule discovery and understand post-merger procurement structure (Next_Step__c).

Close date 16 March 2027 (263 days out) is realistic for a full-suite S2P transformation deal starting post-merger (August 2026 earliest) plus 6-8 month enterprise evaluation, ROI build, demo cycles, and contracting.
High AI appetite confirmed on call: 'I would say so. Yes.' when asked if AI interest is relatively high; Arvind acknowledged Merlin positioning and noted strong ROI as decision driver.
No evidence of cooling or disengagement; the holding pattern is buyer-imposed (merger freeze) and time-bounded, not a ghost scenario.
north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified-stage transformation deal in planned holding pattern; buyer-requested 2-month re-engagement window honored by rep (3 June outreach), now awaiting merger clarity expected early August. 23 days quiet is normal given legal limbo and explicit buyer ask to circle back post-resolution.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage transformation deal with strong AI appetite and clear pain, but single-threaded and blocked by external merger freeze. Post-merger (early August 2026), the deal must move fast to multi-thread (EB, Legal, InfoSec), map Nexstar's S2P strategy and any incumbent threat, and build a quantified ROI case anchored to onboarding and analytics pain. Champion development (Arvind's post-merger role and advocacy) is the lynchpin; if he is displaced, Zycus loses its only thread.

competitive_position.summary: JAGGAER and Ariba are known S2P alternatives from Arvind's prior-company experience (NCR, Equifax, Encompass), not from an active TEGNA evaluation. No evidence of a live competitive process today. The true competitive threat is UNKNOWN until post-merger: if Nexstar has an S2P incumbent (Ariba, JAGGAER, Coupa, or another), that vendor may be imposed on TEGNA, eliminating a selection process. Current incumbent is Oracle Fusion ERP (eProcurement via Punch-Out) and iClient CLM, both acknowledged as painful. Zycus' wedge is AI differentiation (Merlin agentic platform, guided onboarding, iRisk, orchestration in Teams, autonomous negotiation agent) and integrated-suite positioning versus point-solution integration headaches. Arvind confirmed one-stop-shop appeal but made ROI and cost the decision gate. August 2026 post-merger discovery must uncover Nexstar's S2P stack and whether a competitive RFP will occur.

competitors.quote: We have Orca Fusion for ERP... We do our Punch Out Checkout through the Fusion app.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: Oracle Fusion eProcurement is basic Punch-Out only (no intake orchestration, no guided buying, no AI). Position Zycus Merlin intake in Teams as a modern, conversational procurement experience layered on top of Oracle ERP (API integration), eliminating user friction without ripping out the ERP.

competitors.name: iClient CLM (incumbent)
competitors.quote: The current Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) system has several issues and is far from perfect.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: iClient is acknowledged as broken. Position Zycus iContract CLM with Merlin AI contract authoring, risk flagging, and renewal alerts as a transformational upgrade; tie it to the integrated S2P suite narrative (CLM + sourcing + eProcurement in one platform, no integration tax).

competitors.name: Do-nothing / manual spreadsheets (incumbent analytics)
competitors.quote: Do you do analytics? It's all done manually.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
competitors.how_we_win: Manual analytics is the status quo. Position Zycus Spend Analysis with AI-powered category insights, savings tracking, and real-time dashboards as the ROI unlock Arvind needs to justify the transformation investment. Quantify time savings (FTE hours freed from spreadsheet wrangling) in the August ROI model.

items.grounding_quote: Ask Arvind for 1–2 data points (e.g., supplier onboarding volume, time spent on analytics) to tailor an ROI view.

ai_positioning_strength.score: strong interest, not yet validated through demo/POC
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and well-received. Arvind confirmed high AI interest ('I would say so. Yes.') and engaged positively when Zycus positioned Merlin agentic AI, guided onboarding, iRisk supplier monitoring, orchestration in Teams, and autonomous negotiation agent. Zycus emphasized AI leadership (32 patents since 2002, Merlin platform launched 2018, Gartner/Forrester/IDC leader recognition), and Arvind did not object or flag skepticism. However, AI narrative has not yet been tested through a demo, POC, or use-case validation. The August 2026 follow-up must anchor AI ROI to TEGNA's pain (onboarding friction, manual analytics) with quantified productivity gains to move from interest to conviction.

items.quote: If there is an ROI we can drive, and increase sourcing productivity, yeah, certainly.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Strong ROI and measurable productivity gains (sourcing and procurement efficiency)

items.quote: There is benefit to do the one stop shop. It depends on the cost and the ROI we can build.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Integrated one-stop-shop S2P suite (avoid point-solution integration headaches)

items.quote: I would say so. Yes. [when asked if AI interest is relatively high]
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: High interest in AI to enhance sourcing and procurement processes

items.quote: The current Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) system has several issues and is far from perfect.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Fix CLM issues (contract authoring, approval, repository)

items.quote: Onboarding is done in Oracle. Yeah. It does leave something to be desired.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Improve supplier onboarding experience (reduce friction, guided workflow)

items.quote: Do you do analytics? It's all done manually.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Replace manual analytics with real-time spend visibility and insights

items.quote: Do you guys use Microsoft Teams currently in your environment? Yes. [Zycus positioned Merlin intake in Teams; Arvind did not object or flag misalignment]
items.source: Avoma discovery call 7 April 2026, Arvind Swamy
items.criterion: Procurement intake and orchestration embedded in Microsoft Teams

customer_expectations_fit.summary: TEGNA's stated expectations align well with Zycus' AI-differentiated, integrated-suite positioning. Arvind prioritizes ROI/productivity gains, one-stop-shop simplicity, and AI capabilities—all Zycus strengths. Pain areas (CLM friction, onboarding inefficiency, manual analytics) map directly to iContract, iSupplier with guided onboarding + iRisk, and Spend Analysis. Merlin orchestration in Microsoft Teams (which TEGNA uses) addresses the intake/procurement-request friction. The fit is strong on paper, but not yet validated through a demo, POC, or BRD alignment. Post-merger, Nexstar's S2P strategy and any incumbent vendor preference could override TEGNA's fit assessment.

hard.ais_why:
• TEGNA is already leveraging AI apps for invoicing and account payments as part of post-merger transformation with Nexstar. Active AP-adjacent AI deployment lines up with AP Smart Desk.
• Buyer Arvind wants quantified ROI on manual analytics rather than a generic full-suite demo. Pragmatic AI buyer asking for value math.
• Opportunity created April 2026 with recent activity. AI-driven AP transformation is core to the merger thesis, keeping the AI conversation warm even without an Avoma call yet.
hard.next_step:
6/3/26 MQ Reaching out to Arvind to schedule discovery and understand what their procurement process looks like post-merger.


5/18/26 MQ Reach out to Arvind first week of June to check status of Merger with Nextdoor and plan for procurement dept. structure of combined entity.


  • Schedule a follow-up meeting for ~2 months out (as Arvind suggested) and position it as a focused discussion on supplier onboarding and manual analytics ROI, not a generic full-suite demo. 
  • Before that meeting, send brief materials on Zycus’ onboarding and spend analytics capabilities and ask Arvind for 1–2 data points (e.g., supplier onboarding volume, time spent on analytics) to tailor an ROI view based on questionairre.

deltas.subject: Single-threaded ghost risk: Only Arvind Swamy (Head of Procurement) has engaged; no Legal (WILLIAM T HERMAN, Assistant General Counsel), InfoSec, IT (Kurt Rao, SVP & CTO; Nicole Keach, CDO & CTO), Finance (Victoria Harker, CFO; Daniel Caldwell, VP Financial Shared Services), or C-level sponsor introduced. 0 buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles on the opp. If Arvind is displaced post-merger or loses decision influence, Zycus has no alternative thread. The August 2026 post-merger call must secure introductions to at least 2-3 additional stakeholders (CFO/VP Finance for budget authority, Legal for contracting, IT for integration/InfoSec) to de-risk the single point of contact.

deltas.to: Champion at risk due to merger uncertainty: Arvind's role and function post-acquisition are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...')
deltas.subject: Champion at risk due to merger uncertainty: Arvind's role and function post-acquisition are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). If Nexstar absorbs TEGNA procurement or installs its own Head of Procurement, Arvind may lose decision authority or leave the organization, eliminating Zycus' only advocate. The August 2026 call must assess whether Arvind survived the merger and retains influence; if he is out or sidelined, Zycus must identify and engage the new decision-maker (likely a Nexstar procurement leader) immediately.

deltas.to: Post-merger competitive wildcard: If Nexstar has an incumbent S2P vendor (JAGGAER, Ariba, Coupa, or another), that vendor may be imposed on TEGNA, eliminating a
deltas.subject: Post-merger competitive wildcard: If Nexstar has an incumbent S2P vendor (JAGGAER, Ariba, Coupa, or another), that vendor may be imposed on TEGNA, eliminating a competitive selection process and making this deal unwinnable. The August 2026 discovery call must uncover: (1) Nexstar's current S2P stack and vendor relationships, (2) whether Nexstar will standardize on a single platform for the combined entity, (3) whether TEGNA will be allowed to run an independent vendor selection, and (4) what procurement governance model (centralized Nexstar, federated, or fully absorbed) will apply. If Nexstar has a locked incumbent, qualify out early rather than chase a phantom opportunity.

value.flag: Champion at risk due to merger uncertainty: Arvind's role and function post-acquisition are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). If Nexstar absorbs TEGNA procurement or installs its own Head of Procurement, Arvind may lose decision authority or leave the organization, eliminating Zycus' only advocate. The August 2026 call must assess whether Arvind survived the merger and retains influence; if he is out or sidelined, Zycus must identify and engage the new decision-maker (likely a Nexstar procurement leader) immediately.

packets.subject: Champion at risk due to merger uncertainty: Arvind's role and function post-acquisition are uncertain ('if they decide to retain me and my function. If not...'). If Nexstar absorbs TEGNA procurement or installs its own Head of Procurement, Arvind may lose decision authority or leave the organization, eliminating Zycus' only advocate. The August 2026 call must assess whether Arvind survived the merger and retains influence; if he is out or sidelined, Zycus must identify and engage the new decision-maker (likely a Nexstar procurement leader) immediately.

value.flag: Post-merger competitive wildcard: If Nexstar has an incumbent S2P vendor (JAGGAER, Ariba, Coupa, or another), that vendor may be imposed on TEGNA, eliminating a competitive selection process and making this deal unwinnable. The August 2026 discovery call must uncover: (1) Nexstar's current S2P stack and vendor relationships, (2) whether Nexstar will standardize on a single platform for the combined entity, (3) whether TEGNA will be allowed to run an independent vendor selection, and (4) what procurement governance model (centralized Nexstar, federated, or fully absorbed) will apply. If Nexstar has a locked incumbent, qualify out early rather than chase a phantom opportunity.
